title: Toy Giant Hasbro Hit by Cyberattack, Disrupting Global Operations
context: Hasbro confirmed a significant cyberattack that forced several corporate systems offline. The intrusion, detected in late March, has impacted order processing and shipping. While no group has claimed responsibility, experts suggest a ransomware-driven motive aimed at data theft and operational extortion.

title: Google Patches Exploited Chrome Zero-Day Vulnerability
context: Google released an emergency update for Chrome (v146.0.7680.177) to fix CVE-2026-5281, a high-severity 'use-after-free' flaw in the Dawn graphics component. This vulnerability is actively being exploited in the wild to execute arbitrary code via malicious HTML pages.

title: European Commission Investigates 350GB Data Theft
context: The European Commission's AWS cloud environment was breached, resulting in the theft of 350GB of data from the Europa.eu portal. The ShinyHunters group has claimed responsibility, leaking screenshots of internal employee data and email server access as proof.

title: Operation Alice Shuts Down 373,000 Dark Web Domains
context: In a massive global operation, Europol and German authorities dismantled a network of over 373,000 dark web domains. The network primarily hosted fraudulent 'Cybercrime-as-a-Service' (CaaS) offerings and fake CSAM packages used to defraud thousands of users worldwide.

title: QualDerm Data Exposure Affects 3.1 Million Patients
context: Healthcare provider QualDerm reported a massive data exposure incident affecting more than 3.1 million individuals. The leaked information includes sensitive patient PII and health records, marking one of the largest healthcare breaches of the quarter.

title: Axios NPM Package Hit by North Korean Supply Chain Attack
context: Security researchers discovered a malicious version of the popular 'axios' NPM package. The breach is attributed to North Korean state-sponsored actors aiming to inject backdoors into downstream developer environments and corporate networks.

title: Apple Issues Rare Security Patch for Older iOS 18 Devices
context: Despite the push for iOS 26, Apple released a critical security patch for iOS 18 users. The patch addresses a zero-day vulnerability being used in targeted attacks against users who have not yet upgraded to the latest hardware or software versions.

title: Texas School District Locked Out by Ransomware Attack
context: Alamo Heights Independent School District in Texas suffered a total network lockout starting March 23. The attack disabled Gmail and internet services across all campuses, forcing the district to operate on manual business continuity plans.

title: Alleged RedLine Malware Administrator Extradited to US
context: Following a multi-year international manhunt, the lead administrator of the RedLine Infostealer malware has been extradited to the United States. RedLine has been responsible for stealing millions of credentials and financial records globally.

title: US Charges Hacker in Uranium Crypto Exchange Breach
context: The US Department of Justice has unsealed charges against a hacker involved in the breach of the Uranium cryptocurrency exchange. The attacker successfully siphoned millions in digital assets using a smart contract exploit that was recently traced via blockchain analytics.

title: 1 Million Records Leaked from GTA V RP Communities
context: A misconfigured server exposed nearly one million records from several Grand Theft Auto V role-play (RP) communities. The leak includes real-world identities, email addresses, and IP logs of players, posing a high risk of doxxing and swatting attacks.

title: New DeepLoad Malware Spotted in 'ClickFix' Phishing Attacks
context: A new malware strain dubbed 'DeepLoad' is being distributed via social engineering campaigns that trick users into fixing 'browser errors' by running PowerShell commands. Once executed, the malware provides full remote access to the attacker.

title: FBI Warns of Data Risks from China-Made Mobile Apps
context: The FBI issued a fresh warning regarding security risks associated with certain mobile applications developed in China. The advisory highlights concerns over mandatory data sharing with foreign intelligence services and the potential for embedded surveillance features.

title: Dell and HP Roll Out Quantum-Resistant Firmware Updates
context: In a preemptive strike against future threats, Dell and HP have begun rolling out quantum-resistant device security for their enterprise laptop lines. The updates implement new cryptographic standards designed to withstand attacks from future quantum computers.

title: Phishing Campaign Spreads AGEWHEEZE via CERT-UA Impersonation
context: A mass phishing campaign is currently impersonating the Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA). The emails contain malicious attachments that, when opened, deploy AGEWHEEZE malware to exfiltrate sensitive government data.
